note_data.title: Man and Health
keyPoints: Health is a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity
Disease is any condition which interferes with the normal functioning of the body or mind
Communicable diseases are those diseases which can spread from one person to another through various means
Non-communicable diseases are those diseases which do not spread from one person to another
Pathogen is any disease-causing microorganism such as bacteria, virus, fungi or protozoa
Vector is an organism that carries disease-causing microorganisms from infected to healthy individuals
Antibiotics are chemical substances produced by microorganisms that kill or inhibit the growth of other microorganisms
Vaccination is the process of introducing weakened or dead pathogens into the body to develop immunity
Personal hygiene includes cleanliness of body, clothes, teeth, hair and surroundings to prevent diseases
Environmental sanitation involves proper disposal of waste, clean water supply and control of disease vectors

definitions.definition: Health is a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity
Disease is any condition which interferes with the normal functioning of the body or mind
Pathogen is any disease-causing microorganism such as bacteria, virus, fungi or protozoa
Antibiotics are chemical substances produced by microorganisms that kill or inhibit the growth of other microorganisms
Vaccination is the process of introducing weakened or dead pathogens into the body to develop immunity against specific diseases

importantQuestions.question: Differentiate between communicable and non-communicable diseases.
importantQuestions.answer: Communicable diseases can spread from one person to another through air, water, food or direct contact (e.g., tuberculosis, malaria). Non-communicable diseases do not spread from person to person (e.g., diabetes, heart disease).

importantQuestions.question: Write a detailed note on prevention and control of communicable diseases.
importantQuestions.answer: Prevention of communicable diseases includes: (i) Personal hygiene - regular bathing, clean clothes, hand washing (ii) Environmental sanitation - proper sewage disposal, clean water supply (iii) Vector control - elimination of breeding places of mosquitoes and flies (iv) Vaccination - immunization against diseases like polio, measles (v) Isolation of patients - separating infected individuals (vi) Use of antibiotics - for bacterial infections (vii) Health education - creating awareness about disease prevention.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a communicable disease?
Anopheles mosquito is a vector for:

commonMistakes: Students confuse communicable and non-communicable diseases - remember communicable means 'can spread'
Writing incomplete definitions - always write the complete textbook definition word by word
Mixing up vectors and pathogens - vector carries the pathogen, pathogen causes the disease
Forgetting to mention that antibiotics work only against bacteria, not viruses
Not drawing proper labels in diagrams of disease transmission cycles
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1-2 short questions in every BSEK paper
Definition of health appears in 90% of papers - memorize it exactly
Disease prevention methods question is very common - prepare detailed points
Always draw and label diagrams for malaria and dengue transmission cycles
Learn examples of Pakistani context diseases like dengue in Karachi, malaria in Sindhi rural areas
note_data.keyDiagram: Life cycle of malaria parasite showing transmission between Anopheles mosquito and human host - must include labels for sporozoites, liver stage, blood stage, gametocytes, and mosquito gut stages
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define health according to WHO and list factors affecting human health
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (6 marks) — required explanation of communicable and non-communicable diseases with two examples each
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (5 marks) — asked to describe the role of balanced diet in maintaining good health
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q6 (3 marks) — definition of malnutrition and its types

workedExample.question: Define health according to WHO. Explain any three factors that affect human health. (7 marks)
steps: Write the exact WHO definition of health from STBB textbook
List three factors affecting health clearly
Explain each factor with proper detail as per textbook
workedExample.answer: Health according to WHO: 'Health is a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity.' Three factors affecting human health are: 1) Nutrition - Proper balanced diet provides essential nutrients for body functions and prevents malnutrition diseases. 2) Environment - Clean air, water and surroundings prevent spread of diseases and infections. 3) Personal hygiene - Regular cleanliness practices like bathing, brushing teeth prevent bacterial and viral infections.
workedExample.markScheme: WHO definition (2 marks), listing three factors (1 mark), explaining each factor correctly (4 marks total)

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers about health instead of the exact WHO definition from STBB textbook
Avoid mixing communicable and non-communicable disease examples - clearly categorize them as asked in the question
When explaining factors affecting health, provide specific examples rather than vague statements to earn full marks
note_data.quickRecap: Health is WHO-defined complete physical, mental, social well-being. Communicable diseases spread through contact/vectors while non-communicable don't spread. Balanced diet provides essential nutrients preventing malnutrition. Personal hygiene and clean environment are crucial for disease prevention.
